name: Youtube Gallery
body: ### About Youtube Gallery This jQuery plugin displays your Youtube videos as gallery with (or without) Fancybox integration. ### Copyrights This plugin is totally free to personal and comercial use. Use it the way as you want. If you improve the plugin, please let me know! ### Authors and Contributors This plugin was developed by Lucas Moreira (@moreirapontocom). ### Support or Contact Having trouble with the plugin? Send me an e-mail at . ### How to use **Step 1:** include the plugin files ` ` **Step 2:** initiate the plugin without options `` _#gallery:_ videos container **Step 3:** The HTML markup <div id="gallery"> <div class="yt-gallery" data-video-id="[VIDEO ID 1]"></div> <div class="yt-gallery" data-video-id="[VIDEO ID 2]"></div> <div class="yt-gallery" data-video-id="[VIDEO ID ...]"></div> <div class="yt-gallery" data-video-id="[VIDEO ID N]"></div> </div> **OPTIONAL PARAMETERS:** cover: [STRING] thumbnail image (see extras below) videoInfo: [BOOLEAN] display date and video author openInFancybox: [BOOLEAN] display video at new page (Youtube) or play in Fancybox autoplay: [BOOLEAN] autoplay video (only if openFancybox is true) texts: { post_date: [STRING] default text to date, post_author: [STRING] default text to author }, messages: { error: [STRING] default text to error } **EXTRAS** **HOW YOUTUBE THUMBNAIL WORKS** XML : https://gdata.youtube.com/feeds/api/videos/[VIDEO ID]?v=2 EMBED : <iframe width="340" height="191" src="//www.youtube.com/embed/[VIDEO ID]" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe> **THUMBNAILS** (h x w) 90 x 120 http://i1.ytimg.com/vi/dcZNLCUGE2c/default.jpg > default quality 180 x 320 http://i1.ytimg.com/vi/dcZNLCUGE2c/mqdefault.jpg > medium quality 360 x 480 http://i1.ytimg.com/vi/dcZNLCUGE2c/hqdefault.jpg > high quality 480 x 640 http://i1.ytimg.com/vi/dcZNLCUGE2c/sddefault.jpg > slow quality 90 x 120 http://i1.ytimg.com/vi/dcZNLCUGE2c/1.jpg > default quality 90 x 120 http://i1.ytimg.com/vi/dcZNLCUGE2c/2.jpg > default quality 90 x 120 http://i1.ytimg.com/vi/dcZNLCUGE2c/3.jpg > default quality`
note: Don't delete this file! It's used internally to help with page regeneration.
